title: Integration using long division
description: Find indefinite integrals of rational functions by using polynomial long division.
topic_path: Integrating functions using long division and completing the square

hints: We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{2x+1}{x+2}=2-\dfrac{3}{x+2}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x+1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(2-\dfrac{3}{x+2}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int2\,dx-3\int\dfrac{1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=2x-3\text{ln}|x+2|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x+1}{x+2}\,dx=2x-3\text{ln}|x+2|+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{3x+4}{x+3}=3-\dfrac{5}{x+3}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{3x+4}{x+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(3-\dfrac{5}{x+3}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int3\,dx-5\int\dfrac{1}{x+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=3x-5\text{ln}|x+3|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{3x+4}{x+3}\,dx=3x-5\text{ln}|x+3|+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x+4}{2x+6}=\dfrac12+\dfrac{1}{2x+6}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x+4}{2x+6}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac12+\dfrac{1}{2x+6}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\dfrac12\,dx+\int\dfrac{1}{2x+6}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac12x+\dfrac{\text{ln}|2x+6|}{2}+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x+4}{2x+6}\,dx=\dfrac12x+\dfrac{\text{ln}|2x+6|}{2}+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x-2}{3x+6}=\dfrac13-\dfrac{4}{3x+6}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can further manipulate the result as follows:

$\dfrac13-\dfrac{4}{3x+6}=\dfrac13-\dfrac43\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+2}$

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x-2}{3x+6}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac13-\dfrac{4}{3x+6}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac13-\dfrac43\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+2}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\dfrac13\,dx-\dfrac43\int\dfrac{1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac13x-\dfrac43\cdot\text{ln}|x+2|+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac13x-\dfrac{4\text{ln}|x+2|}{3}+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x-2}{3x+6}\,dx=\dfrac13x-\dfrac{4\text{ln}|x+2|}{3}+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x-5}{-2x+2}=-\dfrac12-\dfrac{4}{-2x+2}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can further manipulate the result as follows:

$-\dfrac12-\dfrac{4}{-2x+2}=-\dfrac12+\dfrac{2}{x-1}$

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x-5}{-2x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(-\dfrac12-\dfrac{4}{-2x+2}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(-\dfrac12+\dfrac{2}{x-1}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=-\displaystyle\int\dfrac12\,dx+2\int\dfrac{1}{x-1}\,dx
\\\\
&=-\dfrac12x+2\text{ln}|x-1|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x-5}{-2x+2}\,dx=-\dfrac12x+2\text{ln}|x-1|+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x+3}{4x+4}=\dfrac14+\dfrac{2}{4x+4}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can further manipulate the result as follows:

$\dfrac14+\dfrac{2}{4x+4}=\dfrac14+\dfrac12\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+1}$

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x+3}{4x+4}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac14+\dfrac{2}{4x+4}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac14+\dfrac12\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+1}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\dfrac14\,dx+\dfrac12\int\dfrac{1}{x+1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac14x+\dfrac12\cdot\text{ln}|x+1|+C
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\dfrac14x+\dfrac{\ln|x+1|}{2}+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x+3}{4x+4}\,dx=\displaystyle\dfrac14x+\dfrac{\ln|x+1|}{2}+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x+4}{5x+5}=\dfrac15+\dfrac{3}{5x+5}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can further manipulate the result as follows:

$\dfrac15+\dfrac{3}{5x+5}=\dfrac15+\dfrac35\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+1}$

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x+4}{5x+5}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac15+\dfrac{3}{5x+5}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac15+\dfrac35\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+1}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\dfrac15\,dx+\dfrac35\int\dfrac{1}{x+1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac15x+\dfrac35\text{ln}|x+1|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x+4}{5x+5}\,dx=\dfrac15x+\dfrac35\text{ln}|x+1|+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x-1}{2x+4}=\dfrac12-\dfrac{3}{2x+4}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can further manipulate the result as follows:

$\dfrac12-\dfrac{3}{2x+4}=\dfrac12-\dfrac32\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+2}$

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x-1}{2x+4}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac12-\dfrac{3}{2x+4}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(\dfrac12-\dfrac32\cdot\dfrac{1}{x+2}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\dfrac{1}{2}\,dx-\dfrac{3}{2}\int\dfrac{1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}x-\dfrac{3}{2}\text{ln}|x+2|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x-1}{2x+4}\,dx=\displaystyle\dfrac12x-\frac32\ln|x+2|+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x^3+x}{x+1}=x^2-x+2-\dfrac{2}{x+1}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^3+x}{x+1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(x^2-x+2-\dfrac{2}{x+1}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx-\int x\,dx+2\int 1\,dx-2\int\dfrac{1}{x+1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-\dfrac{x^2}{2}+2x-2\text{ln}|x+1|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^3+x}{x+1}\,dx=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-\dfrac{x^2}{2}+2x-2\text{ln}|x+1|+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{2x^3-3x^2-3x+2}{x-2}=2x^2+x-1$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x^3-3x^2-3x+2}{x-2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(2x^2+x-1\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=2\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx+\int x\,dx-\int 1\,dx
\\\\
&=2\dfrac{x^3}{3}+\dfrac{x^2}{2}-x+C
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\dfrac{2x^3}3+\dfrac{x^2}2-x+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x^3-3x^2-3x+2}{x-2}\,dx=\displaystyle\dfrac{2x^3}3+\dfrac{x^2}2-x+C$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x^3-1}{x+2}=x^2-2x+4-\dfrac{9}{x+2}$

[[☃ explanation 4]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^3-1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(x^2-2x+4-\dfrac{9}{x+2}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx-2\int x\,dx+4\int 1\,dx-9\int\dfrac{1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-2\dfrac{x^2}{2}+4x-9\text{ln}|x+2|+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-x^2+4x-9\text{ln}|x+2|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^3-1}{x+2}\,dx
\\\\
=&\dfrac{x^3}{3}-x^2+4x-9\text{ln}|x+2|+C
\end{align}$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{3x^3-5x^2+10x-3}{3x+1}=x^2-2x+4-\dfrac{7}{3x+1}$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{3x^3-5x^2+10x-3}{3x+1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(x^2-2x+4-\dfrac{7}{3x+1}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx-2\int x\,dx+4\int 1\,dx-7\int\dfrac{1}{3x+1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-2\dfrac{x^2}{2}+4x-7\cdot\dfrac{\text{ln}|3x+1|}{3}+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-x^2+4x-\dfrac{7\text{ln}|3x+1|}{3}+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle\int\dfrac{3x^3-5x^2+10x-3}{3x+1}\,dx
\\\\
=&\dfrac{x^3}{3}-x^2+4x-\dfrac{7\text{ln}|3x+1|}{3}+C
\end{align}$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x^3-3x^2+5x-3}{x-1}=x^2-2x+3$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^3-3x^2+5x-3}{x-1}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(x^2-2x+3\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx-2\int x\,dx+3\int 1\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-2\dfrac{x^2}{2}+3x+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}-x^2+3x+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^3-3x^2+5x-3}{x-1}\,dx
\\\\
=&\dfrac{x^3}{3}-x^2+3x+C
\end{align}$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{2x^3+7x^2+2x+9}{2x+3}=x^2+2x-2+\dfrac{15}{2x+3}$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x^3+7x^2+2x+9}{2x+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(x^2+2x-2+\dfrac{15}{2x+3}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx+2\int x\,dx-2\int 1\,dx+15\int\dfrac{1}{2x+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}+2\cdot\dfrac{x^2}{2}-2x+15\cdot\dfrac{\text{ln}|2x+3|}{2}+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^3}{3}+x^2-2x+\dfrac{15\text{ln}|2x+3|}{2}+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x^3+7x^2+2x+9}{2x+3}\,dx
\\\\
=&\dfrac{x^3}{3}+x^2-2x+\dfrac{15\text{ln}|2x+3|}{2}+C
\end{align}$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{2x^3+4x^2-5}{x+3}=2x^2-2x+6-\dfrac{23}{x+3}$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x^3+4x^2-5}{x+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(2x^2-2x+6-\dfrac{23}{x+3}\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=2\displaystyle\int x^2\,dx-2\int x\,dx+6\int 1\,dx-23\int\dfrac{1}{x+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=2\cdot\dfrac{x^3}{3}-2\cdot\dfrac{x^2}{2}+6x-23\text{ln}|x+3|+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac{2x^3}{3}-x^2+6x-23\text{ln}|x+3|+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle\int\dfrac{2x^3+4x^2-5}{x+3}\,dx
\\\\
=&\dfrac{2x^3}{3}-x^2+6x-23\text{ln}|x+3|+C
\end{align}$
We can rewrite the integrand using polynomial long division, because the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator.

After performing the division, we obtain the following result:

$\dfrac{x^6+2x^4+6x-9}{x^3+3}=x^3+2x-3$

[[☃ explanation 3]]

Now we can perform the integration:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^6+2x^4+6x-9}{x^3+3}\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int\left(x^3+2x-3\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\displaystyle\int x^3\,dx+2\int x\,dx-3\int 1\,dx
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^4}{4}+2\dfrac{x^2}{2}-3x+C
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x^4}{4}+x^2-3x+C
\end{align}$

In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle\int\dfrac{x^6+2x^4+6x-9}{x^3+3}\,dx
\\\\
=&\dfrac{x^4}{4}+x^2-3x+C
\end{align}$
